---
title: Heat Conduction
---

> Auto-generated 2026-05-06 19:23 UTC &nbsp;·&nbsp; 42 plots

Quasi-2D steady heat-conduction compliance minimisation on a heated slab with SIMP material penalisation (p=3). The effective conductivity k_eff(ρ) = k_min + (k_max − k_min)·ρ³ controls heat routing; the compliance C = ∮_Γ q_n·T dΓ is the work done by the heat flux on the temperature field. The hot-spot boundary condition (central 1/3 stripe) breaks y-symmetry and drives topology optimisation toward non-trivial branching structures. Also supports source-identification experiments: recover the volumetric heat source f(x) from temperature observations via the identification_error = ||T − T_target||² objective.

::: {.callout-note title='Boundary conditions'}

Quasi-2D heated slab on domain [0,2]×[0,1] (nz=1 HEX8 layer). Dirichlet: all nodes at x=0 held at T=0 (fixed temperature). Neumann (uniform): uniform heat flux Q_total over the right face (x=2). Neumann (hot-spot): flux concentrated on the central 1/3 stripe in y (Ly/3 ≤ y ≤ 2Ly/3) at the right face, driving non-trivial topology.

:::

## Initial Conditions

Visualisation of each initial condition available for this problem. IC plots are generated without running any solver.

### Gaussian Source

Gaussian heat source centred at (cx·Lx, cy·Ly) = (0.5·Lx, 0.5·Ly) with width σ·min(Lx,Ly). Used as the control field for source-identification experiments (ic_field='source' in physics dict).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/ics/gaussian_source/ic.png){.lightbox}

### Random

Gaussian-noise density field centred at ρ₀=0.5 (σ=0.3, clipped to [0.05, 0.95]); breaks spatial symmetry to produce non-trivial per-cell gradient sensitivity maps.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/ics/random/ic.png){.lightbox}

### Uniform

Uniform SIMP thermal conductivity density ρ₀ over all hex mesh elements; standard homogeneous starting point for heat-conduction topology optimisation.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/ics/uniform/ic.png){.lightbox}

## Forward

Forward-pass benchmarks: agreement between solvers, field diagnostics, and long-run stability.

### Agreement

Near-void contrast test: thermal compliance vs element density ρ₀ sweeping from near-void (ρ=0.01) to near-solid (ρ=0.95). SIMP p=3 creates orders-of-magnitude conductivity contrast; FV and FEM handle near-void channels differently.

Thermal compliance C vs uniform element density ρ₀ ∈ [0.01, 0.95] at N=16. C ∝ ρ⁻³ due to SIMP (p=3); near-void (ρ→0) divergence between FV harmonic-mean and FEM Galerkin conductivity is the key discriminator. Log scale recommended.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `rho_0` ∈ {0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 0.95}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "uniform",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 16,
    "ny": 8,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "rho_0",
    "values": [
      0.05,
      0.1,
      0.2,
      0.4,
      0.6,
      0.8,
      0.95
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/agreement/curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/agreement/errors.png){.lightbox}

### Baseline

FV vs FEM discretisation divergence: thermal compliance vs mesh resolution N with heterogeneous random density. Non-uniform ρ stresses FV harmonic-mean vs FEM Galerkin conductivity interpolation; phase transition visible at coarse N.

Thermal compliance C vs mesh resolution N (nx=2,3,4,6,8,12,16,24; ny=nx//2; nz=1) with random density ρ~N(0.5,0.3) clipped to [0.05,0.95]. FV solvers diverge from FEM at coarse N due to harmonic-mean vs Galerkin conductivity interpolation; gap closes as O(h) with refinement. N=2–4 is the phase-transition regime.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `N` ∈ {2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "random",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "N",
    "values": [
      2,
      3,
      4,
      6,
      8,
      12,
      16,
      24
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/baseline/curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/baseline/convergence.png){.lightbox}

### Physical Laws

C ∝ Q² scaling law with hot-spot BC. Uniform flux would be trivially symmetric; hot-spot (central 1/3 stripe) breaks y-symmetry and tests flux-area handling.

Thermal compliance C vs total heat flux Q_total at fixed N=16, ρ₀=0.5, hot_spot=True. For a linear system C ∝ Q² (log-log slope 2.0). Hot-spot BC concentrates flux on central 1/3 stripe in y, breaking symmetry; deviations across solvers reveal errors in Neumann mask handling or compliance integral.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `Q_total` ∈ {0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 4.0}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "uniform",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "N": 16,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5,
    "hot_spot": true
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "Q_total",
    "values": [
      0.25,
      0.5,
      1.0,
      2.0,
      4.0
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/physical_laws/physical_laws.png){.lightbox}

### Source Baseline

Resolution convergence for source identification: temperature field L2 error vs N with a Gaussian source. Uses ic_field='source' so the IC is the heat-source field rather than the density.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `N` ∈ {4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "gaussian_source"
  },
  "physics": {
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5,
    "ic_field": "source"
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "N",
    "values": [
      4,
      6,
      8,
      12,
      16,
      24
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/source_baseline/curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/source_baseline/errors.png){.lightbox}

### Source Linearity

Linearity check: T(α·f) = α·T(f). Sweep source amplitude α. For a linear PDE the temperature scales linearly with the source amplitude.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `amplitude` ∈ {0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 4.0}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "gaussian_source"
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 16,
    "ny": 8,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5,
    "ic_field": "source"
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "amplitude",
    "values": [
      0.1,
      0.25,
      0.5,
      1.0,
      2.0,
      4.0
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/source_linearity/curves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/forward/source_linearity/errors.png){.lightbox}

## Gradient

Gradient benchmarks: finite-difference checks and gradient quality (magnitude, direction cosine) across parameter, resolution, and horizon sweeps.

### Finite-Difference Check

FD gradient check vs analytic VJP at nominal mesh and heat flux.

U-curves (FD gradient error vs ε), direction cosine between AD and FD gradient vectors, and gradient magnitude field panels.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "random",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 8,
    "ny": 4,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      1.0,
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001,
      0.0001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 6
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/fd_check/fd_check.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/fd_check/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}

### Parameter Sweep

Gradient quality vs element density ρ₀ at fixed mesh.

Gradient norm, best-ε FD error, direction cosine, and U-curves vs element density ρ₀.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `rho_0` ∈ {0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "uniform",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 8,
    "ny": 4,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      1.0,
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001,
      0.0001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 6
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "rho_0",
    "values": [
      0.1,
      0.2,
      0.4,
      0.6,
      0.8
    ]
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/param_sweep/error_vs_param.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/param_sweep/param_sweep.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/param_sweep/ucurves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/param_sweep/best_eps_vs_param.png){.lightbox}

### Differentiability Table

FD differentiability check for all array inputs and outputs.

Differentiability table: status (ok/fail/not_differentiable) and relative FD error for each (input field, output field) pair. rho is the primary differentiable input; hex_mesh and boundary_conditions are non-differentiable structs.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "random",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 4,
    "ny": 2,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps": 0.001,
    "n_dirs": 1
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/differentiability_table/differentiability_table.png){.lightbox}

### Jacobian Svd

Jacobian SVD and gradient subspace analysis at nominal mesh.

Singular-value spectrum of the stacked per-solver gradient matrix and pairwise cosine similarity between JAX-FEM and FEniCS gradient directions for the thermal compliance objective. Near-unity cosine confirms consistent adjoint implementations; spectrum reveals dominant sensitivity modes of the density field.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "random",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 8,
    "ny": 4,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0
  },
  "jacobian": {
    "n_alphas": 21,
    "alpha_range": 0.2
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/jacobian_svd/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/jacobian_svd/jacobian_svd.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/jacobian_svd/landscape.png){.lightbox}

### Source Fd Check

FD gradient check of d(identification_error)/d(source) at nominal mesh. Uses ic_field='source' and output_key='identification_error'.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "gaussian_source"
  },
  "ic_key": "source",
  "output_key": "identification_error",
  "physics": {
    "nx": 8,
    "ny": 4,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5,
    "target_from_two_gaussians": true,
    "ic_field": "source"
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      1.0,
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001,
      0.0001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 6
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/source_fd_check/fd_check.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/source_fd_check/gradient_fields.png){.lightbox}

### Source Width Sweep

Gradient quality vs source localisation σ. Phase transition: FEM/FD disagree as source narrows below element size.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

Sweeps `sigma` ∈ {0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "gaussian_source"
  },
  "ic_key": "source",
  "output_key": "identification_error",
  "physics": {
    "nx": 16,
    "ny": 8,
    "nz": 1,
    "rho_0": 0.5,
    "target_from_two_gaussians": true,
    "ic_field": "source"
  },
  "fd": {
    "eps_values": [
      0.1,
      0.01,
      0.001,
      0.0001
    ],
    "n_dirs": 4
  },
  "sweep": {
    "key": "sigma",
    "values": [
      0.05,
      0.1,
      0.2,
      0.3,
      0.5
    ],
    "ic_sweep": true
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/source_width_sweep/error_vs_param.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/source_width_sweep/param_sweep.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/source_width_sweep/ucurves.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/gradient/source_width_sweep/best_eps_vs_param.png){.lightbox}

## Optimization

Optimization benchmarks: gradient-based recovery of initial conditions or physical parameters, topology optimization, and drag minimization.

### Conductivity Recovery

Recover a two-Gaussian conductivity field from temperature observations. Optimises rho (SIMP density, clipped to [x_min, 1]) to minimise identification_error = ||T(rho) - T_target||². Target temperature is produced by forward-solving with a two-Gaussian ground-truth conductivity at uniform zero volumetric source (driven by Neumann BC only).


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "uniform",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 16,
    "ny": 8,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5,
    "Q_total": 1.0,
    "compliance_key": "identification_error",
    "penalty_weight": 0.0,
    "x_min": 0.001,
    "snap_interval": 20,
    "target_rho_from_two_gaussians": true
  },
  "optim": {
    "lr": 0.01,
    "max_iters": 2000,
    "patience": 200
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery/conductivity_recovery_convergence.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery/conductivity_recovery_evolution_dealii_heat.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery/conductivity_recovery_evolution_fenics_heat.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery/conductivity_recovery_evolution_firedrake_heat.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery/conductivity_recovery_evolution_jax_fem.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery/conductivity_recovery_evolution_torch_fem_thermal.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery/conductivity_recovery_fields.png){.lightbox}

### Conductivity Recovery Bfgs

Recover a two-Gaussian conductivity field with L-BFGS. Same setup as conductivity_recovery but using L-BFGS with zoom line search.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

```json
{
  "ic": {
    "name": "uniform",
    "seed": 0
  },
  "physics": {
    "nx": 16,
    "ny": 8,
    "nz": 1,
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5,
    "Q_total": 1.0,
    "compliance_key": "identification_error",
    "penalty_weight": 0.0,
    "x_min": 0.001,
    "snap_interval": 10,
    "target_rho_from_two_gaussians": true
  },
  "optim": {
    "max_iters": 200,
    "patience": 30
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery_bfgs/conductivity_recovery_convergence.png){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery_bfgs/conductivity_recovery_evolution_fenics_heat.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery_bfgs/conductivity_recovery_evolution_firedrake_heat.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery_bfgs/conductivity_recovery_evolution_jax_fem.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery_bfgs/conductivity_recovery_evolution_torch_fem_thermal.gif){.lightbox}
![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/optimization/conductivity_recovery_bfgs/conductivity_recovery_fields.png){.lightbox}

## Cost

Wall-clock cost scaling with problem size N and number of integration steps.

Wall-clock and memory profiling vs mesh size for all solvers.


::: {.callout-note collapse='true' title='Settings'}

**Spatial Cost**

Sweeps N ∈ {16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4500}

```json
{
  "physics": {
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5
  },
  "cost": {
    "N_values": [
      16,
      32,
      64,
      128,
      256,
      512,
      1024,
      2048,
      4500
    ],
    "n_trials": 3
  }
}
```

**Vjp Cost**

Sweeps N ∈ {16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4500}

```json
{
  "physics": {
    "Lx": 2.0,
    "Ly": 1.0,
    "Lz": 1.0,
    "Q_total": 1.0,
    "rho_0": 0.5
  },
  "cost": {
    "N_values": [
      16,
      32,
      64,
      128,
      256,
      512,
      1024,
      2048,
      4500
    ],
    "n_trials": 3
  }
}
```

:::

![](../mosaic-results/thermal-mesh/cost/cost.png){.lightbox}
